name: Return to the Guadalhorce Reservoir - circular from El Chorro
name_original: Vuelta al Embalse del Guadalhorce – circular desde El Chorro
region_intro: Touring cycling around Álora offers diverse landscapes, from the fertile Guadalhorce Valley to the dramatic El Chorro Gorge. The region features routes traversing gentle rural hills, enclosed rocky areas, and serene inland water bodies, including the Guadalhorce Lakes. Cyclists can explore mountainous surroundings like the Sierra del Valle del Abdalajís and Sierra de Aguas, providing varied terrain and panoramic views. Best touring cycling routes around Álora The most popular touring cycling route is Return to the Guadalhorce Reservoir - circular from El Chorro, a 31.7 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Guadalhorce Reservoir and the El Chorro area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Álora Castle Loop Trail, a moderate 15.0 miles (24.1 km) path. This route provides access to the historic Álora Castle, offering panoramic views over the Guadalhorce Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Bar El Pilar – Abfahrt in Richtung Ardales loop from El Chorro-Caminito del Rey, a 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail leading through the El Chorro area, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Álora is defined by fertile valleys, dramatic gorges, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Álora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 1,300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Álora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling around Álora?
A: Touring cycling around Álora offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll traverse the fertile Guadalhorce Valley, featuring gentle rural hills and the Green Corridor with its extensive cycling routes. The region also includes dramatic areas like the El Chorro Gorge (Desfiladero de los Gaitanes) and the stunning turquoise waters of the Guadalhorce, Guadalteba, and Conde Guadalhorce reservoirs, often called the Ardales lakes. Expect views of narrow rock walls and ascents into mountain ranges such as the Sierra del Valle del Abdalajís and Sierra de Aguas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Álora?
A: While Álora is known for its more challenging routes, there are options for various abilities. The region has 7 moderate routes, which are suitable for those with good fitness. Currently, there are no routes specifically classified as easy, but some sections of the Guadalhorce Valley's Green Corridor offer gentler terrain. For those finding the hills challenging, e-bikes are readily available for rent in the area.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints accessible by touring bike in Álora?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Álora offer access to significant landmarks and breathtaking viewpoints. You can cycle to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980957194/alora-castle-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides magnificent panoramic views over the Guadalhorce Valley. The Mirador Alí Ben Falcún “Al Baezi” viewpoint below the castle also offers sweeping vistas. Routes often pass by the iconic Caminito del Rey gorge, offering views of this impressive natural feature, and lead to the beautiful Guadalhorce Lakes.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Álora area?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes around Álora. A popular option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974393886/return-to-the-guadalhorce-reservoir-circular-from-el-chorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Guadalhorce Reservoir - circular from El Chorro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the reservoir and the El Chorro area. Another is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980957194/alora-castle-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Castle Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, providing access to the historic castle.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Álora?
A: The touring cycling routes in Álora are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the fertile valleys to the dramatic gorges and mountain ranges, and the stunning views of the Guadalhorce Lakes and historic sites like Álora Castle.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs I can cycle to or around in Álora?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its stunning inland water bodies. Many routes lead to or offer views of the Guadalhorce, Guadalteba, and Conde Guadalhorce reservoirs, collectively known as the Ardales lakes. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaitanejo Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2785359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de la Encantada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3782093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conde de Guadalhorce Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, all offering picturesque cycling opportunities.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Álora?
A: While not explicitly stated in the guide, the Mediterranean climate of Andalusia generally makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the landscapes are vibrant, offering comfortable conditions for longer rides. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though still often suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Álora?
A: Yes, Álora offers plenty of challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. Out of 31 routes, 24 are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain and longer distances. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974393886/return-to-the-guadalhorce-reservoir-circular-from-el-chorro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Guadalhorce Reservoir - circular from El Chorro&lt;/a&gt; (51.1 km with 977m elevation gain) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1274778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel to the Caminito del Rey – Guadalhorce Reservoir loop from Las Mellizas&lt;/a&gt; (53.8 km with 1049m elevation gain) provide demanding rides through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find bike rentals or guided tours in Álora?
A: Yes, the region's infrastructure supports touring cyclists with options for bike rentals, including hybrid bikes and e-bikes, which can be particularly helpful for tackling the steeper hills. Several companies also offer guided cycling tours and holidays, providing local knowledge and support to enhance your experience in the area.

Q: Are there any interesting historical or cultural sites to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Álora and its surroundings are rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980957194/alora-castle-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álora Castle&lt;/a&gt;, with its Roman and Moorish origins, is a prominent cultural landmark. You can also discover unique sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4203187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozarabic Rock-Cut Church of Bobastro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5794400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliff Dwellings on the Guadalhorce River&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the region's historical heritage and can be integrated into your cycling adventures.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Álora?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes available around Álora, offering a wide range of experiences. These routes cater to different fitness levels, with a majority being classified as difficult, alongside several moderate options.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through other traditional 'pueblos blancos' near Álora?
A: Yes, many cycling routes in the region connect Álora with other charming traditional 'pueblos blancos' (whitewashed villages) such as Ardales, Carratraca, and Pizarra. This allows cyclists to immerse themselves in authentic Andalusian culture and architecture while enjoying the scenic rides.
tips:
From this point, you have a beautiful view of the Caminito del Rey suspension bridge, which spans the Rio Guadalhorce gorge. The road is quiet with little traffic, and the spot is easily accessible.

If you then choose the route across the dam bridge, you can also drive on the other side of the Embalse Tajo de la Encantada reservoir. This route takes you via El Chorro even closer to the mouth of the gorge. There are two options: either along the water's edge or up the path almost to the Caminito del Rey exit. For the latter, it's advisable to wait until the Caminito del Rey closes due to the crowds, as the path is a steep, gravel climb. The view, however, is even more magnificent.

Next to the current hermitage, which was renovated in the 1960s, prehistoric tombs from the Mozarabic period were discovered, carved into the rock. Near the hermitage, on a promontory, the remains of a watchtower known as the Peñón del Moro were discovered, evidence of the great importance of the enclave at that time.

Almost picturesque, how the pavilion on the steep slope fits into the landscape of the Embalse del Conde de Guadalhorce reservoir. Maybe a bit cheesy, but the proportions of the picture are almost perfect. Be sure to stop and take a picture!
Great way to go from Bobadilla to Embalses de Guadalhorce-Guadalteba

Along the road you always have such fantastic views of the Embalse del Guadalhorce and the beautiful mountain region around it. A paradise for climbers, hikers and paragliders! And of course for cyclists too!
